1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	- archon-coordinator-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-coordinator-agent.py` — decompose a multi-part directive and dispatch children in parallel
24	- archon-review-*-agent: five read-only review subagents (`review-definition-correctness`, `review-comment-hygiene`, `review-blueprint-consistency`, `review-design-choices`, `review-mathlib-overlap`) that audit one aspect of the project each. Typically dispatched from the review phase.
25	
26	All subagent tools share the same argument shape: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path> [--write-domain '<glob>']...`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
27	
28	## Key Files & Permissions
29	
30	All state files are in `.archon/`:
31	
32	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
33	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
34	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
35	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
36	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
37	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
39	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
40	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
41	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
42	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
43	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
44	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
45	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
46	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
47	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
48	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
49	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
50	
51	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
52	
53	## Protected Declarations
54	
55	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
56	
57	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
58	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
59	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
60	
61	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
62	
63	The blueprint uses two active markers.
64	
65	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
66	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
67	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
68	
69	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
70	
71	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
72	
73	## User Interaction
74	
75	Users provide hints in two places:
76	
77	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
78	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
79	
80	## Agent Roles
81	
82	### Plan Agent
83	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
84	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
85	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
86	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
87	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
88	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
89	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
90	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
91	
92	### Prover Agent
93	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
94	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
95	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
96	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
97	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
98	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
99	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
100	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
101	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
102	
103	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
104	
105	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
106	
107	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
108	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
109	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
110	
111	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
112	
113	### Review Agent
114	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
115	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
116	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
117	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
118	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
119	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
120	
121	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
122	
123	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
124	
125	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
126	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
127	
128	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Review Agent — Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis
2	
3	You are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) maintain the blueprint markers that require *semantic* judgement (`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations), and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	`\leanok` placement is handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs immediately before you. **Do not add or remove `\leanok` yourself.** If a `\leanok` you expect is missing, the underlying Lean source still has a sorry or doesn't compile — investigate, don't paper over.
6	
7	**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs.** You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts as part of your analysis. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint-marker files.
8	
9	## Iteration number — canonical
10	
11	Your invocation prompt contains `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. As of 2026 these are the **same number** — `session_M/` is always the review of `iter-NNN` (i.e. M == NNN). Use the iteration form (`iter-NNN`) when referencing the iteration in prose (`summary.md` metadata, recommendations titles like "Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-{NNN+1:03d})") and the bare integer (`session_M`) when referring to the review-output directory. If older `summary.md` / `recommendations.md` files use the legacy independent session counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents — your new file uses the iteration number.
12	
13	## Step 1: Identify Context
14	
15	1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` — count existing session folders to determine the current session number.
16	2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.
17	3. Run `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` to see what changed.
18	
19	## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)
20	
21	**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.
22	
23	The file contains:
24	
25	- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)
26	- Remaining lines: One event per tool call — edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds
27	
28	**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text → new_text).
29	**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.
30	**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.
31	**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.
32	
33	This is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.
34	
35	## Step 3: Read Recent History
36	
37	If previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
38	
39	## Step 4: Write Proof Journal
40	
41	Create the session folder and write two files:
42	
43	```bash
44	mkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>
45	```
46	
47	### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`
48	
49	Must include:
50	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)
51	- For EACH target attempted:
52	  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point
53	  - What was learned from each failed attempt
54	  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas
55	- Key findings / proof patterns discovered
56	- Recommendations for next session
57	
58	### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`
59	
60	Each line MUST follow this JSON format — one entry per target theorem:
61	
62	```json
63	{
64	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
65	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
66	  "target": {
67	    "file": "path/to/File.lean",
68	    "theorem": "theorem_name"
69	  },
70	  "session": {
71	    "id": "session_N",
72	    "model": "model-name"
73	  },
74	  "findings": {
75	    "blocker": "description if blocked",
76	    "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1", "lemma2"]
77	  },
78	  "attempts": [
79	    {
80	      "attempt": 1,
81	      "strategy": "what was tried",
82	      "code_tried": "actual Lean code or tactic",
83	      "line_number": "line number in the initial file where this code was attempted",
84	      "lean_error": "actual error message if failed",
85	      "goal_before": "the goal state before this attempt",
86	      "goal_after": "the goal state after this attempt",
87	      "result": "success|failed|partial",
88	      "insight": "what was learned from this attempt"
89	    }
90	  ],
91	  "next_steps": "..."
92	}
93	```
94	
95	**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:
96	- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded
97	- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)
98	- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one
99	
100	### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`
101	
102	Write concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:
103	- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized
104	- Which approaches showed promise but need more work
105	- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)
106	- Any reusable proof patterns discovered
107	
108	If your analysis shows the prover has hit the exact same blocker for several consecutive iterations on the same target, you should explicitly instruct the Plan Agent to avoid retrying the same approach without putting more effort into understanding the underlying issue.
109	
110	## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md (Knowledge Base only)
111	
112	The per-session narrative (Overall Progress: total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, this session's analysis) goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` — see the next subsection. PROJECT_STATUS.md carries only the cumulative Knowledge Base:
113	
114	```markdown
115	# Project Status
116	
117	## Knowledge Base
118	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
119	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
120	
121	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
122	- <target>: <reason>
123	
124	## Last Updated
125	<ISO timestamp>
126	```
127	
128	If an existing PROJECT_STATUS.md still carries an "Overall Progress" section from a prior project that grew it, leave the old content where it is (do not delete history) but stop appending to it. New session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
129	
130	### Per-iteration sidecars (split write)
131	
132	Your invocation prompt contains a `## Per-iteration sidecars` block that names `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` as the destination for THIS session's narrative. Split your writes accordingly:
133	
134	- **The per-session "Overall Progress" narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**, NOT to PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Overall Progress" section. PROJECT_STATUS.md no longer carries a session log.
135	- **You DO keep updating PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Knowledge Base" section.** Cumulative non-obvious facts (errors not to reproduce, reusable proof patterns, Mathlib idioms that worked) still belong in the Knowledge Base. The Knowledge Base is the only growing-but-curated part of PROJECT_STATUS.md.
136	- **Format for `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**: same fields as the Overall Progress block above (Total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, plus any narrative analysis). Born-bounded — this file contains THIS session only, never a multi-session log.
137	- Older `iter/iter-MMM/review.md` files are on disk for you to read on demand, but the recent window is already injected in your prompt.
138	
139	## Step 6: Blueprint Markers
140	
141	`\leanok` placement is now handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs between the prover and you. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration in the Lean source, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. **Do not touch `\leanok` markers yourself.** If you see one missing where you expect it, the underlying file probably doesn't compile or still has a sorry — do not paper over that with a manual edit.
142	
143	Your remaining marker responsibilities are the ones that require semantic judgement:
144	
145	- **`\mathlibok`** (statement-block only) — declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias / direct reference). The deterministic script never adds or removes this; you decide based on the prover's task result.
146	- **`\lean{...}` macro maintenance** — if a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `\lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.
147	- **`% NOTE: <reason>` annotations** — when a block is unformalized because the informal statement did not translate cleanly, add a `% NOTE: ...` comment explaining the obstacle so the plan agent sees it.
148	- **Stripping `\notready`** — if a `\notready` marker still sits on a block that the prover has now landed, remove it. The deterministic script does not manage `\notready`.
149	
150	### `\mathlibok` rules (your domain)
151	
152	Add `\mathlibok` inside the statement block when:
153	- The Lean side references a Mathlib name directly (`def foo := Mathlib.bar`, `theorem foo := Mathlib.bar`, or a simple `export Mathlib.Foo (bar)`), AND
154	- The Archon-side declaration itself contains no `sorry` and introduces no new proof obligation.
155	
156	If you add `\mathlibok`, no `\leanok` is needed on the proof block — the deterministic script will leave the proof block alone if there's no Lean proof body to verify.
157	
158	### Record what you changed
159	
160	In `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated" section listing **only the changes you personally made** (the deterministic script's `\leanok` adds/removes are committed separately as `archon[NNN/marker-sync]` and don't need to appear here):
161	
162	```markdown
163	## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
164	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
165	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: added `% NOTE: prover reported translation gap, see task_results/Core.md`
166	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
167	- `Core.tex`, `thm:foo`: corrected `\lean{Old.foo}` → `\lean{New.foo}` after refactor rename
168	```
169	
170	### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`
171	
172	While the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. 
173	
174	**Rules:**
175	- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).
176	- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.
177	
178	## Step 7 (optional): Dispatch review subagents
179	
180	You may dispatch any of five specialized review subagents BEFORE writing your consolidated summary. They are read-only audits whose findings you incorporate into `summary.md` / `recommendations.md`. Each runs as its own fresh-context Claude process; spawning two or more in one assistant message runs them in parallel, subject to the global `max_parallel` cap.
181	
182	The five reviewers:
183	
184	- **`review-definition-correctness`** — flags stand-in / mathematically-wrong definitions (the LineBundle failure mode). Use when this session introduced or modified `def` blocks.
185	- **`review-comment-hygiene`** — flags iter-history comments in source, stale TODOs, docstring/body drift. Cheapest; useful every session.
186	- **`review-blueprint-consistency`** — verifies Lean↔blueprint `\lean{...}` references resolve and signatures match. Use after refactors or when sync_leanok reports unexpected drift.
187	- **`review-design-choices`** — flags parallel pipelines, re-derivations of Mathlib API, suboptimal definitional choices. Heaviest reasoning; use when the session added substantial new infrastructure.
188	- **`review-mathlib-overlap`** — narrower than design-choices: scans new files for declarations whose signatures mirror existing Mathlib. Cheap when scope is one file.
189	
190	### How to dispatch
191	
192	Pattern (Bash tool, parallel-able):
193	
194	```
195	python3 .claude/tools/archon-<role>-agent.py \
196	  --slug <kebab-case-slug> \
197	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-directive.md \
198	  --write-domain 'task_results/**'
199	```
200	
201	Each directive is fully self-contained — the reviewer reads only what the directive points it at. See each reviewer's prompt file under `.archon/prompts/<role>.md` for the exact directive shape.
202	
203	### When NOT to dispatch
204

  unfold cechCofaceMap_summand_family
  simp only [Limits.Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
